speaker: IAGO
text: Though in the trade of war I have slain men,

speaker: IAGO
text: Yet do I hold it very stuff o’ th’ conscience

speaker: IAGO
text: To do no contrived murder. I lack iniquity

speaker: IAGO
text: Sometimes to do me service. Nine or ten times

speaker: IAGO
text: I had thought t’ have yerked him here under the ribs.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: ’Tis better as it is.

speaker: IAGO
text: Nay, but he prated

speaker: IAGO
text: And spoke such scurvy and provoking terms

speaker: IAGO
text: Against your Honor,

speaker: IAGO
text: That with the little godliness I have

speaker: IAGO
text: I did full hard forbear him. But I pray you, sir,

speaker: IAGO
text: Are you fast married? Be assured of this,

speaker: IAGO
text: That the magnifico is much beloved,

speaker: IAGO
text: And hath in his effect a voice potential

speaker: IAGO
text: As double as the Duke’s. He will divorce you

speaker: IAGO
text: Or put upon you what restraint or grievance

speaker: IAGO
text: The law (with all his might to enforce it on)

speaker: IAGO
text: Will give him cable.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Let him do his spite.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: My services which I have done the signiory

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Shall out-tongue his complaints. ’Tis yet to know

speaker: OTHELLO
text: (Which, when I know that boasting is an honor,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I shall promulgate) I fetch my life and being

speaker: OTHELLO
text: From men of royal siege, and my demerits

speaker: OTHELLO
text: May speak unbonneted to as proud a fortune

speaker: OTHELLO
text: As this that I have reached. For know, Iago,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: But that I love the gentle Desdemona,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I would not my unhousèd free condition

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Put into circumscription and confine

speaker: OTHELLO
text: For the sea’s worth. But look, what lights come yond?

speaker: IAGO
text: Those are the raisèd father and his friends.

speaker: IAGO
text: You were best go in.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Not I. I must be found.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: My parts, my title, and my perfect soul

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Shall manifest me rightly. Is it they?

speaker: IAGO
text: By Janus, I think no.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The servants of the Duke and my lieutenant!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The goodness of the night upon you, friends.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What is the news?

speaker: CASSIO
text: The Duke does greet you, general,

speaker: CASSIO
text: And he requires your haste-post-haste appearance,

speaker: CASSIO
text: Even on the instant.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What is the matter, think you?

speaker: CASSIO
text: Something from Cyprus, as I may divine.

speaker: CASSIO
text: It is a business of some heat. The galleys

speaker: CASSIO
text: Have sent a dozen sequent messengers

speaker: CASSIO
text: This very night at one another’s heels,

speaker: CASSIO
text: And many of the Consuls, raised and met,

speaker: CASSIO
text: Are at the Duke’s already. You have been hotly called for.

speaker: CASSIO
text: When, being not at your lodging to be found,

speaker: CASSIO
text: The Senate hath sent about three several quests

speaker: CASSIO
text: To search you out.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: ’Tis well I am found by you.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I will but spend a word here in the house

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And go with you.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Ancient, what makes he here?

speaker: IAGO
text: Faith, he tonight hath boarded a land carrack.

speaker: IAGO
text: If it prove lawful prize, he’s made forever.

speaker: CASSIO
text: I do not understand.

speaker: IAGO
text: He’s married.

speaker: CASSIO
text: To who?

speaker: IAGO
text: Marry, to—

speaker: IAGO
text: Come, captain, will you go?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Have with you.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Here comes another troop to seek for you.

speaker: IAGO
text: It is Brabantio. General, be advised,

speaker: IAGO
text: He comes to bad intent.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Holla, stand there!

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Signior, it is the Moor.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Down with him, thief!

speaker: IAGO
text: You, Roderigo! Come, sir, I am for you.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Keep up your bright swords, for the dew will rust them.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Good signior, you shall more command with years

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Than with your weapons.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: O, thou foul thief, where hast thou stowed my daughter?

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Damned as thou art, thou hast enchanted her!

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: For I’ll refer me to all things of sense,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: If she in chains of magic were not bound,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Whether a maid so tender, fair, and happy,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: So opposite to marriage that she shunned

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: The wealthy curlèd darlings of our nation,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Would ever have, t’ incur a general mock,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Run from her guardage to the sooty bosom

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Of such a thing as thou—to fear, not to delight!

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Judge me the world, if ’tis not gross in sense

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: That thou hast practiced on her with foul charms,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Abused her delicate youth with drugs or minerals

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: That weakens motion. I’ll have ’t disputed on.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: ’Tis probable, and palpable to thinking.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: I therefore apprehend and do attach thee

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: For an abuser of the world, a practicer

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Of arts inhibited and out of warrant.—

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Lay hold upon him. If he do resist,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Subdue him at his peril.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Hold your hands,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Both you of my inclining and the rest.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Were it my cue to fight, I should have known it

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Without a prompter.—Whither will you that I go

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To answer this your charge?

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: To prison, till fit time

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Of law and course of direct session

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Call thee to answer.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What if I do obey?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: How may the Duke be therewith satisfied,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Whose messengers are here about my side,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Upon some present business of the state,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To bring me to him?

speaker: OFFICER
text: ’Tis true, most worthy signior.

speaker: OFFICER
text: The Duke’s in council, and your noble self

speaker: OFFICER
text: I am sure is sent for.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: How? The Duke in council?

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: In this time of the night? Bring him away;

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Mine’s not an idle cause. The Duke himself,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Or any of my brothers of the state,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Cannot but feel this wrong as ’twere their own.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: For if such actions may have passage free,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Bondslaves and pagans shall our statesmen be.
